Off Calendar May-07-2025 Continued To Dec-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA WARD's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement.

Plaintiff Cynthia Ward's unopposed Motion for Preliminary Approval of Class Action Settlement is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court: (1) grants preliminary approval of the proposed Class Action Settlement Agreement ("Settlement"); (2) conditionally certifies the proposed Class for settlement purposes only; (3) appoints Plaintiff as the Class Representative; (4) appoints The Ottinger Firm, P.C. as Class Counsel; (5) appoints CPT Group to serve as the Settlement Administrator; (6) approves as to form and content the Notice of Class Action Settlement; and (7) sets a final approval hearing date for November 10, 2025 at 9:00 am in this department, for which Plaintiff shall file a memorandum identifying and discussing outstanding issues no later than October 31, 2025, with courtesy copy delivered to the department within 24 hours of filing.

Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Decertify Class
Off Calendar Aug-04-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STT LINES, INC.'S Motion To Decertify Class.

Defendant STT Lines, Inc.'s "motion to decertify class" is denied without prejudice. Though filing a motion to decertify a class before one is certified causes cognitive dissonance, California law apparently allows it. (See City of San Jose (1974) 12 Cal.3d 447, 453-54.) However, plaintiff must first be permitted discovery on her "joint employer" theory. (See Bartold v. Glendale Federal Bank (2001) 81 Cal.App.4th 816, 827.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
